title: Document-Section Item
description: Validates per-item document-section YAML in content-generation Framework Skills. Mirrors the control-schema envelope (id, title, description, applicability, evidenceHints) and adds content-generation fields (template, templatePath, templateFormat, inputs, selectWhen, attestation). Items MUST supply exactly one of 'template' (inline body) or 'templatePath' (path to a file under docs/templates/).

template.description: Inline markdown body for the section; may contain {{var}} references resolved against inputs and manifest globals. Mutually exclusive with templatePath.

templatePath.description: Repo-relative path to an external template file under docs/templates/. Mutually exclusive with template. The host loads file content at render time; for markdown the FSI variable-resolution lint scans the file body for {{var}} tokens, transparently treating Docusaurus-escaped \{\{var\}\} forms as resolvable tokens.

templateFormat.description: Format of the external template referenced by templatePath. Only 'markdown' is rendered by the v1 host renderer; 'docx' and 'pptx' are reserved for future renderer skills (existence-only validation today).

inputs.description: Per-section variable declarations consumed by the template

discriminator.description: Short label for the applicability axis

appliesWhen.description: Discriminator values under which this section applies

naWhen.description: Discriminator values under which this section is not applicable

naReason.description: Reason text recorded when this section is skipped under naWhen

applicability.description: Optional applicability discriminator mirroring the control schema

evidenceHints.description: File paths or globs that provide context for this section

discriminator.description: Short label for the variant axis (e.g. prd_template_variant); resolved against host project context

values.description: Discriminator values that include this section (positive selection)

notValues.description: Discriminator values that exclude this section (negation symmetry with applicability.naWhen)

selectWhen.description: Positive-selection predicate distinct from applicability; an item is included when discriminator value matches values (and does not match notValues). Items without selectWhen are always included.

attestation.description: Optional per-item attestation contract declaring which artifacts produced for this item MUST carry an attestation signature. Hosts honor the manifest-level signing block to determine method and identity; this per-item block scopes coverage. The signing-attestation consistency lint warns when 'covers' references identifiers that resolve to neither a pipeline.stages[].produces[].id nor a known item id within the same bundle.

required.description: When true, hosts MUST attach an attestation for each id listed in 'covers' before declaring the item complete.

covers.description: Identifiers covered by the attestation. Each entry SHOULD resolve to either a pipeline.stages[].produces[].id (preferred for binary outputs) or a sibling item id within the same bundle.

reviewer.description: Required reviewer role (for example 'legal counsel', 'WCAG auditor', 'security architect').

disclaimer.description: Optional per-item advisory disclaimer that overrides metadata.disclaimer when set. Hosts surface this text alongside generated content. Cross-link: .github/instructions/shared/disclaimer-language.instructions.md for canonical planner phrasing.

name.description: Variable name referenced in {{var}} template tokens

description.description: Human-readable description of the input variable

required.description: Whether the host must collect a value before rendering

persistence.description: Value retention scope. 'none' = re-prompt every render; 'session' = remember within a single host session (default); 'project' = persist in the consuming project (e.g. .copilot-tracking/); 'user' = persist in user-scope storage. The persistence-sanity lint warns when 'user' is paired with sensitive: true.

sensitive.description: Marks the input value as sensitive (PII, credentials, etc.). Hosts SHOULD redact in logs and SHOULD NOT persist beyond session scope. Drives the persistence-sanity lint.
